1. The selection borders swapped (left panel now blue, right panel now gray)
2. The charcoal shape on the left panel moved from rows 8-11 to row 8 (just a small shape at the top left)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = history[-2].frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Changes after clicking left selection panel ===")
for r in range(len(before)):
    if before[r] != after[r]:
        print(f"Row {r}:")
        print(f"  Before: {before[r]}")
        print(f"  After:  {after[r]}")

# Also check the charcoal shape position
print("\n=== Charcoal shape on left panel ===")
for r in range(7, 13):
    row = after[r][7:32]
    if 'c' in row:
        print(f"Row {r}: {row}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I clicked the left selection panel:
1. The charcoal shape disappeared from rows 8-11 (where it was before)
2. A new charcoal shape appeared at rows 16-19 on the left panel
3. The selection borders swapped (left panel now blue, right panel now gray)
4. The right panel's first horizontal bar changed from BBB back to www
5. The left panel's vertical bars at rows 47-49 changed from B back to w
So the selection panels control which shape is displayed on the left panel:
- Right panel selected → charcoal shape at rows 8-11 (upper position)
- Left panel selected → charcoal shape at rows 16-19 (lower position)
This is very revealing! The two selection panels correspond to the two yellow objects on the right panel:
- Right selection panel (yellow bar shape) → upper yellow object position
- Left selection panel (2 yellow blocks shape) → lower yellow object position
Now I need to figure out what the buttons do. When I selected the right panel, the left panel's vertical bars at rows 47-49 changed to B. When I selected the left panel, they changed back to w.
Let me also check: the right panel's first horizontal bar changed from www to BBB when I clicked it earlier. This suggests the buttons toggle states.
The goal might be to:
1. Select each panel in turn
2. Use the buttons to adjust something
3. Click the player to submit
But I'm not sure what the buttons do yet. Let me try clicking the player now with the left panel selected to see if it advances the level.

</parameter>
